publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-01-16 19:48:20 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Documentation spanning 1976-2019 of Page Hodel's (b. December 4th, 1965) work to create, promote, and DJ at dance parties, clubs, festivals, and fundraisers designed to welcome diverse audiences in the San Francisco Bay Area and beyond. The collection consists of typed and handwritten correspondence and notes, agreements and applications (for instance, to have a float in a Pride Parade), and many kinds of event promotional material, including postcards, fliers, posters, brochures, programs, photographs taken to use in publicity, mock-ups and drafts of such promotional material, and clippings, newspapers, magazines and books that contain references to these events, interviews and articles about Page Hodel's clubs and activity. Also, negatives, slides, and some photocollages Hodel created, memorabilia, and digital material on zip drives and CDs.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Page Hodel papers document the professional activities of the dance club DJ, radio Mixtress, and nightclub promoter Page Hodel (b. December 4th, 1965), dating from 1976 to 2019, mainly in the San Fransisco Bay Area.

The papers consist of Hodel's working files, such as correspondence, agreements, typed and handwritten notes, postcards, and fliers related to her career as a DJ, the events and clubs she created, her productions, club gigs, and one-off events. Also included are fliers, mock-ups, posters, brochures, programs, magazines, newspapers, books, clippings, and other publicity and promotional material from her clubs and parties; photographs, negatives, and slides; memorabilia such as buttons, badges, party kit bags, stamps, clothing, banners, and other ephemera from events and clubs, particularly from the long-running Club Q and The Box; and digital material, including zip drives and CDs.

The material documents her long career in the music industry, from her notorious birthday parties and the years of DJ booking at Amelia's, Oasis, and other venues to her collaboration with Olivia Cruises in the 1990s (See Series I. Working files). Documents cover her collaborations with KSOL, as the station's first female mixer, in the early 1980s and later with LIVE 105. Extensive material pertains to her nightclub promotion and event producer career, covering Hodel's The Box and Club Q, as well as other parties and venues, such as the Wet, Dance Factory, Eve, Girl Pride, Jump, Soul, and more, as well as DJing at outdoor events, pride parades, and festivals, such as the San Francisco Pride Parade and the Folsom Street Fair. (See Subseries I. C. Events production, I. D. Hodel's events, and clubs; I. E. One-off events).

Her numerous awards and honors, including many Cable Car awards for San Francisco's Favorite DJ and Cable Car Awards Hall of Fame, San Francisco Bay Guardian awards for Best Dance Club for the box, and Best DJ, are also part of the collection. In addition, there is documentation of Hodel's involvement in AIDS, cancer awareness, LGBTQ-focused, and other types of fundraisers, such as the AIDS Dance-a-Thons in San Francisco, New York, and Los Angeles, the AIDS Walk, the Women's Cancer Walk, the San Francisco AIDS Foundation, the Project Inform Volunteer Appreciation Parties and more.

A large portion of the photographs are attributed to photographer Chloe Atkins. Hodel hired her to create portraits of many patrons and staff from The Box, as well as Club Q. Hodel celebrated the beauty of the diverse individuals she attracted and entertained and used these images of the real people coming to the clubs in her promotions. The collection includes Atkins' prints of The Box crowds as well as the publicity material they featured in. Other photographers who contributed are Patrick R. Reynolds, Jessica Tanzer, Dawn Lewis, and others. Photo collages created by Page Hodel are contained in Series IV. Photographs, Slides, and Negatives.

Although The Box was open to all genders and sexualities throughout its 11 years of operation, there are a few letters and articles related to one case where the efforts of The Box's staff to maintain this dance club as a safe and healthy environment for the gay community and their friends got misinterpreted as an act of sex discrimination (see more in Box 1, Folders 24, 25, Box 3, Folder 27, Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 16-17 and for an issue at Club Q, see Box 4, Folder 51).
Finally, Hodel included in her archives a folder related to the election of U.S. President Barack Obama in 2008, including her voter registration card, sample ballots, and more.

The collection does not contain material pertaining to Hodel's project (and later publication, "Monday Hearts for Madelene," images of presents of one-of-a-kind handmade hearts Hodel constructed and photographed weekly for 11 years in honor of her beloved partner Madelene Rodriguez, whom she lost to ovarian cancer a few months after they first met.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Born and raised in the San Francisco Bay Area, D.J. Page Hodel provided the Soul, Funk, Disco, New Wave, Hip Hop, and House soundtracks for the SF Bay Area LGBTQ community across 4 historic decades. In the early 1980s, she became the first female mixer on a major market radio station, KSOL, and started DJ'ing at many clubs (Amelia's, Oasis, and more). In 1987, she founded both The Box, a multicultural dance club for people of all genders and sexualities that ran for 11 years and featured future stars, including Queen Latifah, and Club Q, a monthly dance party for "women and friends" that ran for 16 years. Hodel managed the Dance Stage at San Francisco's annual Folsom Street Fair for its first 8 years and DJ'd at many outdoor festivals and pride parades around the United States and on the first 13 Olivia Cruises.

Hodel has received many awards and honors for her contribution to the music industry. In 1988, Billboard Magazine selected her to serve in the much-coveted "Dance Chart Reporter" position, and for 16 years, Page represented San Francisco as one of the country's one hundred dance chart reporters for Billboard Magazine. Other awards include the Cable Car Awards plaques for Outstanding DJ (1991, 1992, 1993, and 1994) and Outstanding Dance Club (1991, 1992, and 1997), Cable Car Awards for "San Francisco's Favorite DJ", Award AC4A Angel Awardee Lifetime Achievement, and more. She has been involved in numerous fundraising events for AIDS and cancer awareness, LGBTQ-focused, and other events, such as the AIDS Dance-a-Thons in San Francisco, New York, and Los Angeles, the AIDS Walk, the Women's Cancer Walk, the San Francisco AIDS Foundation, Curve Magazine Legal Defense Fund, the Project Inform Volunteer Appreciation Parties, and more.

Page Hodel is a carpenter and small house builder, as well as an artist and musician. She is also an operations manager of Living Jazz, a Bay Area nonprofit whose mission is to "Transform Lives through Music," and a DJ with a full schedule.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Page Hodel papers, #7912. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

archdesc.relatedmaterial:
Related Materials Related material to Box 5, Folder 17 "Kissing Shoot by Jessica Tanzer" may be found in Jessica Tanzer papers, #7866. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Material Online resource in "Page Hodel Presents Saturday Night Dance Party at the Box"

did.unittitle: Series I. Page Hodel Working Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material is overlapping throughout the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subseries title supplied by the donor.

did.unittitle: 37th Annual San Francisco LGBT Pride Celebration

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Playlist from 1997, brochures, buttons, wristbands, technical schedule and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Hodel accepting an offer to be photographed for Leibovitz's book "Women".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional postcard for the benefit for the AIDS Project of the East Bay, held on Sept. 15, 1995.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2000 Project Inform La Fabuloso Award to Page Hodel; Spin it! Dance Event recognizes Page Hodel for tireless efforts in support of the fight against HIV/AIDS(1996).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The International Newsweekly of Music and Home Entertainment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included certifications of Appreciation by the Board of Directors of South of Market Merchants and the Individuals Lifestyle Events (SMMILE) for the Folsom Street Fair (1990), City of Hope, National Medical Center, and Beckman Research Institute, and relevant correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a collaged birthday party invitation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the artist's contract and photographs from the event. Relevant material in Box 2, Folder 89, Box 4, Folder 28, Box 5, Folder 24, and Box 14, Folder 10.

did.unittitle: Business cards, letterheads, timecards and more

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included stationery, job application sheets and more.

did.unittitle: Cable Car Award programs and relevant material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program of 1997: p. 12 "Overture" nomination DJ Page Hodel; p. 16 Hodel's biographical note. Program of 1991: Oustanding Dance Club and DJ (p. 6, 10, 11), and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original folder title "You've got some fucking nerve." Ephemera pertaining to Club Queue, which Hodel alleged was attempting to capitalize on Q's success by using a similar name and advertising in the same newspapers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder title supplied by the donor. The Cube (the name was a play on The Box) was an unrealized idea for a club that would play edgier rock music.

did.unittitle: Correspondence related to AIDS awareness events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains a list of "SF's Most Powerful Women," which includes Hodel, torn out from a magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included Page Hodel's ID card for the 1985 New Music Seminar, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Polaroid of Hodel and a friend in the DJ booth.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual leather/kink fair founded in 1984, also known for its DJ sets and live music. Included photographs, programs, postcards, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper ad for the 1995 Pride Party at the theme park.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence related to 105 Live Radio, NCLR, The Folson Street Fair, and more; also, appreciation letters, a letter from Senator Milton Marks (1988), and a letter to the editor published in a 1990 issue of the Sentinel complaining that the door staff had not let the writer into The Box. For the same event, see also Box 1, Folder 25; Box 3, Folder 27; Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 16-17, 29, 51.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included handwritten postcards, invitations, appreciation letters, and other personal notes to Pade Hodel. There is also a letter from a lawyer alleging that The Box excludes heterosexuals and asking for damages on behalf of his clients who were denied admission. For the same event, see also Box 1, Folder 24; Box 3, Folder 27; Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 16-17, 29, 51.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional postcard for the event, held on May 27, 1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handbills for performances by female punk bands Dogtown and Permanent Wave.

did.unittitle: Letters, invitations, and postcards from friends and clubgoers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a portrait of a woman by photographer Kathryn Khadija Leverette, handbills, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A non-profit that produced San Francisco events benefiting various gay and AIDS-related charities.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page Hodell participated in the event as a musical director/off-stage announcer (see credits at the last page of the program).

did.unittitle: Notes, letters, and requests from clubgoers and friends

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten notes from clubgoers, variously complimenting and complaining about the music, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cruises for LGBTQ women. Included a promotional postcard for a Women's Cruise party held on May 29, 1991, at Club Q, photographs, the official 1999 San Francisco Pride Guide (p. 27 Olivia ad), and more. Relevant material (buttons and badges) can be found in Series V. Memorabilia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Events sponsored by Out magazine, Broadway Cares/Equity Fights AIDS, and DIFFA (Design Industries Foundation Fighting AIDS), benefiting AIDS organizations nationwide. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folder 17 Correspondence related to AIDS awareness events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of the Accident Waiver and Release of Liability Form for the Page Hodel. Archive Project was a project using volunteers from the community to organize Hodel's archives.

did.unittitle: Photographs of club employees in Christmas parties and other gatherings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a flier for Club Q's first-anniversary summer picnic, held on September 3, 1989, and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handbill advertising the 1997 after Pride Party at the Sound Factory, DJ'ed by Hodel and Mariah Hanson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the first check from KSOL and a mini cassette.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: California Scooter making donations to the San Francisco AIDS Foundation from every new scooter they sell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Button from Spin it! Can be found in Series V. Memorabilia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series title supplied by the donor.

did.unittitle: Dance Factory. Artwork by Rive Nestor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in Box 5, Folder 15, Box 6 Memorabilia, Banners by Rive Nestor and stamp, Box 1, Folder 47.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay and lesbian club in Berkeley in the mid-90s. Included hand-drawn drafts for postcards/posters by artist Brian Egg, a handbill urging patrons to boycott Nu Deal records after two men representing the company shouted homophobic abuse while at the Dance Factory, and more. Relevant material can be found in Box 5, Folder 15, and Series V. Memorabilia, Box 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly party for women held at The Factory in SOMA. Included a belt buckle in the shape of the club's logo. Relevant material can be found in Series Clothes, Box 19.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly party for women held at Factory 525 in SOMA. It opened in 2002 and featured 5 rooms, each with a different style of music, plus screenings of films by local women filmmakers. Relevant material in Series Clothes, Box 19.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weekly party for women held at the Cherry Bar in SOMA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weekly party at SOMA club Beatbox. Started in 2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weekly women's party started in 1992. Included photographs. Relevant material can be found in Series V. Memorabilia, Box 6, and in Series Clothes and Banners, Banner Jump, (MU-4443).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Underground, circuit, rave dance party. Opened in 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included truck rental forms, fax machine copies, an accident report (the truck's side panels had come off), and a request for reimbursement for the truck's cost from Levi Strauss (who had sponsored the float).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 64-67, Box 3, Folder 70, Box 9, Folder 2, Box 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also Box 4, Folder 43, The Real Roxanne.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, press releases, public letters, mock-ups, postcards, and a mouse pad.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weekly tea dance held at Harry Denton's Starlight Room starting in 2008.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lesbian bar in Oakland that opened in 2007. Hodel was the promoter but soon moved the event to another bar after a conflict with the owners. Relevant Material at the Digital Material Series, Velvet CD-77355.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ad torn out of a newspaper, featuring photographs by Chloe Atkins. Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 57, 64-67, Box 3, Folder 70, Box 9, Folder 2, Box 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 57, 64-67, Box 3, Folder 70, Box 9, Folder 2, Box 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 57, 64-67, Box 3, Folder 70, Box 9, Folder 2, Box 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Women's tea dance (Sunday afternoon party) held around 1992. Included photographs of partygoers by Chloe Atkins. Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 57, 64-67, Box 3, Folder 70, Box 9, Folder 2, Box 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hodel's production company founded in 1987. Series and folder titles supplied by the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The lesbian magazine was first titled Deneuve but changed its name in 1996 after a legal dispute with actress Catherine Deneuve. This benefit was to help pay for legal costs from the dispute. Included a legal fund benefit for Curve Magazine held on Sunday, May 19, 1996, at City Nights.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcard for an event in honor of Christina Mara Carrillo, a beloved Bay Area DJ who died in a car accident in October 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A 1993 New Year's Eve party sponsored by The Girl Spot and Club Q.

did.unittitle: Handwritten and printed notes on events' production

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fundraiser for Melange, "a film about the mixed race experience." A picture of performer Vicki Randle on printer paper, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Queer music festival held on August 22-24, 2014 in Saratoga Springs, CA. It was intended to be an annual event but only ran for one year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nectar Women's Stage at San Francisco Pride held on June 23, 2002, at Harry Denton's Rouge. It featured an all-female DJ lineup.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Politician elected to the San Francisco Board of Supervisors in 1989.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in the subseries Club Gigs, Folders Pride Parade floats, and Pride Parties.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A benefit for the Women's Cancer Walk in San Francisco.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A benefit for Project Open Hand held on Wednesday, July 1, 1992, at The Box. Included postcards for Sweat, Funky World Party, Escapade, a 1990 dance party sponsored by Asian Pacific Sisters and the Gay Asian Pacific Alliance, a 1991 benefit for Trikone, Asian/Pacifica Sisters, and Gay Asian Pacific Alliance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series and folder titles supplied by the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tropical-themed women's tea dance, held at Bahia Tropical in San Francisco.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lesbian bar in San Francisco's Mission district, open from 1978 to 1991. Included a flier advertising "Last Call" at Amelia's on November 16, 1991 (the club closed the next day).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay bar open from 1998 to 2009.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Guest house, bar, and club for women in San Francisco, open from 1982 to 1987. Relevant material in Box 2, Folder 50, Box 4, Folder 12.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly dance party for women.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Women's dance club in SOMA. Hodel DJ'ed Thursday nights.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly lesbian party in San Francisco, held on April 12, 2008.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Different events at the 18+ hip hop club in SOMA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Club Moon at Nine ran from July 15th to 19th, in either 1988 or 1989. Club Nine was a popular club and aart gallery in San Francisco. Located in an old motel, artists had their own rooms to turn into galleries. Hodel DJed a funk night there.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labor Day weekend party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weekly party held in San Francisco from 1994 to 2002. Included postcards advertising events at the club, including appearances by Chaka Khan and Grace Jones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 18+ Club in SOMA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Revolving lesbian club in the late 1980s created by promoter Caroline Clone.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay club in Guerneville, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lesbian night at KOC bar in Mountain View, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Saturday night lesbian party held at the Endup. Promoter Mariah Hanson also founded Dinah Shore Weekend in Palm Springs. Included ads for the G-Spot events, most from the early 2000s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay bar in San Rafael, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay Asian bar in San Francisco.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fay nightclub in San Francisco's South of Market neighborhood. Relevant material in Box 2, Folders 16, 50; Box 3, Folder 75; Box 4, Folder 12; Box 15, Folder 13.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly dance club for "women and friends" held at various locations in and around Santa Rosa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Plugged In" event featured hip-hop, dancehall, and contemporary music.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included two diagrams for the Box. Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 9, and Box 2, Folder 35.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay bar in Guerneville, CA. Opened in 1979.

did.unittitle: San Francisco Pride Parade and more

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A newspaper clipping reading "Stage 1 (Polk Street dance stage) and a program for an unknown dance event held on Sept. 21, 1986. Yellow cloth with text "Defy the censors make music frees the mind".

did.unittitle: San Francisco LGPT Pride: invoices, licenses and more

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Club's LGBTQ night Unity Thursdays.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Held every second Saturday at Club 21, a lesbian club in Sacramento. The club also hosted Crush every third Friday.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly party in San Francisco's SOMA district. Included postcard advertising a fundraiser for Tom Ammiano's 1994 campaign for the Board of Supervisors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hodel's Thursday night gigs at the Stud, an iconic and long-running San Francisco gay bar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weekly party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly lesbian party at the Cat Club in SOMA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After-hours club in SOMA, opened in 1977 and closed in the late 1990s. Women's dance every other Wednesday.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After-hours club in SOMA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monthly dance party for women held at Medjool in San Francisco.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay club in Guerneville, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series supplied by the donor. Folders contain mainly invitations, postcards, and other publicity material from one-off events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bartenders Bash, held July 29, 1986, at the Trocadero Transfer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 16, 32, Box 2, Folders 16, 50, Box 3, Folder 75, Box 4, Folder 12; Box 15, Folder 13.

did.unittitle: Club Universe, Russian River and Dance the Night Away

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Club Universe was a weekly party held in San Francisco from 1994 to 2002.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of photographer and LGBT activist Cramer by Chloe Atkins.

did.unittitle: Handbills, postcards, programs, and notes from one-off events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a letter welcoming Hodel to a 1999 event sponsored by the Aspen Gay & Lesbian Community Fund. Spectrum's annual Gala and other events.

did.unittitle: National Center for Lesbian Rights (NCLR), Duke March, and Hify

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an NCLR card thanking Hodel for DJ-ing the events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Legendary Bay Area DJ. Signed publicity photograph of Paul from radio station KMEL.

did.unittitle: Postcards, brochures and handbills of one-off events
Programs from Page Hodel's participation in several events
Programs, scripts, memos, playlists from Page Hodel's participation in several events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CLUB LUNACE (1999), New LeaF Soring Gala (2004-2005), NCLR 1998, 2002, 8th Annual SAMMY Awards, 2003, Aretha (1985), and more. Included also relevant correspondence, fliers, proposals, invoices, and more.

did.unittitle: Series II. Publicity and promotional material
II.A. Mock-ups, logos, fliers, and other promotional material
Box (The). Advertisement of a photo shoot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused graphics and flier mock-up and mock-up for a postcard advertising the new ventilation system.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopied pictures of boom boxes, to be used for the club's logo.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Box and the Kennel Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coupons for $1 off entry printed to look like dollar bills with The Box's logo in the center.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Parties included: To spark the fire, The Bay Area's annual pride celebration, Every Thursday night, 9th anniversary, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the cover art for King's single "Flirt".

did.unittitle: Box (The). Every Thursday (Leigh, Ronnie and other fliers)
Box (The). Every Thursday (with William and Paul)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included mock-ups with Paul and William.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sheets of ID tags for float dancers and one mock-up for the tag.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For Saturday parties, and others.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Gay Day, 1992 and Gay R&B

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After Pride Party (1992). Mock-ups for fliers; photographs used on fliers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Club's logo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pride after party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gay Pride Day mock-ups.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier announced The Box's 1-year anniversary party on April 22, 1989, featuring a performance by funk singer Georgio. Mock-ups, photographs, and transparent sheets. Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 10, Box 4, Folder 28, Box 5, Folder 24, and Box 14, Folder 10.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups of a flier announcing the grand opening, held on April 7, 1988. See also Box 3, Folder 6, Box (The). Logo Dancers and Box (The). Box 3, Folder 15. Poem and public letter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sheets of mock-ups of promotional postcards. Plus, Box Boo Halloween Party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slow Jam room; Every Thursday Jams, Jams (Leigh) and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sheets and one cut-out of drawings of people dancing. See also Box 3, Folder 1, Box (The). Grand Opening

did.unittitle: Box (The). Mailing lists and audition sign-ups

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A sheet of mock-ups for a form for club goers to sign up for the mailing list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slips of paper with various phrases to be used on handbills (ie, "Every Thursday," "Grand Opening").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Page Hodel. See also Box 3, Folder 1.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Press Releases, fliers and handwritten notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the announcement of The Box's move back to its original location.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included mock-ups and handbills for various events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8.5x11 inch pieces of cardstock. Several of the photographs used in these postcards, taken by Chloe Atkins.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Public letters and birthday list

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a handwritten draft for a speech at the 7th-anniversary party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Different mock-ups of a postcard announcing Queen Latifah's performance at The Box on Dec. 31, 1990. Relevant material can be found in Box 4, Folder 42, Queen Latifah.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Twister Game, LATRIM, and Changing fliers and contact sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contact sheets by Chloe Atkins.

did.unittitle: Box (The). "Very important please read" flier and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups and handwritten notes for a sign noting that The Box "was created to provide a safe and healthy environment for the gay community and their friends" and is not the place for "curious eyes or angry hands." The sign was posted near the entrance after an SF Chronicle article on the club led to more straight clients. Included a newspaper clipping commenting positively on a readers' letter titled "Let Gay Men In." Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 24, 25; Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 16-17, 29, 51.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Win a boom box Saturday mock-ups
Box (The). Your dance. Promotional photographs, negatives, and mock-ups

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten notes on expenses, names and contact information for dancers, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Halloween cards and illustrations. Relevant material in Memorabilia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups of the fliers for the parties held on Nov. 23, 1990 and May 15, 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For an event on Aug. 25, 1989. Photographs by Chloe Atkins; transparent sheets with text for the flier written on them and mock-ups.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Model Dorice, Photograph by Chloe Atkins.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Every Friday mock-ups and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier and mock-up of a postcard announcing the grand opening on Dec. 29, 1989. After several rotating locations from 1987 to 1989, Club Q was permanently located at 628 Divisidero Street. See also Box 3, Folder 45.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also Box 3, Folder 44.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Letter flier, old antique

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups for the flier show an antique-looking illustration of the letter Q and a cherub.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Membership cards, guess passes, mailing lists and more

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes press releases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-up of fliers for events on February 24, March 31, and June 23, 1989, a flier illustrated with a photo by Chloe Atkins.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Notes and drafts on flier designs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-up of a flier for the first Club Q on Nov. 5, 1987

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups of a flier for the Club Q grand opening on Dec. 29, 1989, featuring a paper doll cut-out competition. Contestants had to draw and cut out a paper doll chain.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Paper doll , Judging Ballot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups for a ballot for the Paper Doll Cut Out Competition, along with photocopies of them. Relevant material Box 6. Club Q, a cut-out paper Qs in a party kid (MU-4257).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After-party flier (May 26, 1989). A sheet of mock-ups of a promotional postcard (April 28, 1989); Little Girl flier (November, 1989); Latti (June, 1991).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On the back is a map of the location of a September 1989 picnic for employees of Club Q and the Box. Party kit inserts for Club Q, including material from Boo kit (Halloween). Relevant Material Folder 3.32 Club Q. Boo kit and Series V. Memorabilia, Club Q, four Party kits MU-4247-4249, 4250.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Valentine's Day party held on Feb. 5, 1990, at Club Q. Included pages of flier designs based on playing cards, transparent sheets, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Valentine Dance party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Valentine Dance party.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Respect, Spirit and Pride handbills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included two handwritten drafts for a voicemail message promoting Spirit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sheet with a "Q" logo and handwritten notes and two photocopies of it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by Darlene Photo Graphics 1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the Wet Memorial Day dance Party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wet, Grand opening and other parties.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jump and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups for a postcard for Hodel's Friday night gigs at the Oasis. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 16, 32, Box 2, Folders 16, 50, Box 4, Folder 12; Box 15, Folder 13.

did.unittitle: Posters and photographs from Club events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the San Francisco Gay Pride Parade proposed plans, designed by Louie Marco and the Box Float Committee (1998); An artwork about Box's three-year anniversary by the artist Joseph de la Roz.

did.unittitle: Ads and references in newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included drafts for an article titled "Back to the Funk: The Rebirth of Cool at the Box" by Stefanie Kelly and a draft of an article titled "Day" about The Box by Elinor Craig.

did.unittitle: Book: Betty, and Pansy. Betty & Pansy's Severe Queer Review of San Francisco. 4th ed. San Francisco, Calif.: Bedpan Productions, 1995.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: p.61 reference to The Box.

did.unittitle: Book: Lewin, Ellen., ed. Inventing Lesbian Cultures in America. Boston, Mass: Beacon Press, 1996.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed by the authors Ellen Lewin (p. 1) and Deborah P. Amory (p. 145) to Page Hodel. Chapter "Club Q: Dancing with (a) Difference," by Deborah P. Amory. p. 145-160.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Ads and entries in newspaper clippings, guides and magazines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included original and photocopies of the article "Bustin' a Move at the Box" By Barry Walters, January 28, 1990 issue of the San Francisco Chronicle, which contributed to Box's visibility for the people out of the gay community. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 24, 25, Box 3, Folder 27, Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 16-17, 29, 51.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Newspaper and magazine clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included, the letter to the community about Club Q closing by Page Hodel (December 2022).

did.unittitle: Hot Shots in the Box and Club Q
Magazines with ads, references and interviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gaybook, Summer/Fall 1990, p. 69 (The Box ad); Curve p. 26 (Curve Lesbian Awards); Rewind v.1 i.8 (March 1993), p. 24 (Page Hodel's interview). Inside Pride 2006. On Q, bay Area magazine, vol.1, numb. 3, p. 9 (Club Q). Uncommon Voices, vol. 21, no.6, July 2000 (Respect ad); Next Step, The international Diversity Guide, vol. 1, no.2, Fall 1996; The City, San Francisco's Magazine, vol. 4, no 2, March 1993.

did.unittitle: National Center for Lesbian Rights Programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20th anniversary (1997); 21st anniversary (1998); 23rd anniversary (2000), (Respect ad, pp. 37); 24th anniversary (2001), (Olivia Cruises and Resorts ad, pp. 22).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ads and references on Page Hodel, Wet Party, the Box, Respect, Club Q, and more.

did.unittitle: Newspapers and magazines with interviews and ads

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the following magazines: Creampuff Magazine, 7/22-8/5/1999, p. 2 (Soul ad); San Francisco Frontiers Magazine, vol. 18, issue 8, August 12, 1999, p.39 (Soul ad); Cashbox Newsletter, August 24, 1985; Whazzup! Magazine, vol. III, Issue 6, October p. 13 (The Box ad); Whazzup! Magazine, vol. II, Issue 8, December 1997 p. 2 (The Box ad). The Charles M. Holmes Campus of the SF LGBT Community Center Grand Opening Week Program Guide , March 2-10, 2001.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included Page Hodel's registration card, sample ballots, and a program from the Oakland Mayoral Inaugural celebration (2007).

did.unittitle: Newspaper and magazine articles and ads

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "All is not smooth at Velvet" by Heather Cassell. Bay Area Reporter, vol. 37, no 15, 12 April 2007 p. 1, 16. Inside Pride magazine, the official SF pride guide p. 87 (The Box ad), Spunk! vol.2 issue 1 January 1998, p. 1 (The Box ad); Cashbox Newspaper, August 24, 1985, Aspen Gay and Lesbian Ski week program 1999. Female FYI, issue 36, August 1996

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included articles regarding the San Francisco Chronicle article on The Box, which resulted in an "invasion" of straight people trying to get into the club. Material relevant to this event can be found in Box 1, Folders 24, 25, Box 3, Folder 27, Box 4, Folders 2, 16-17, 29, 51.

did.unittitle: Newspaper and magazine clippings related to AIDS awareness events
Newspaper clippings on several clubs and events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Baybrick Reunion, Eve, Curve, Soul, Russian River, Wet, Pride Parage, Oasis, DNA Lounge, Anthem and more. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 16, 32, Box 2, Folders 16, 50, Box 3, Folder 75, Box 4, Folder 12; Box 15, Folder 13.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue 2, March 1995, issue 4, June 1995, p. 9 (Article about the Box); vol 2, issue 1 Feb. 12-March 14, 1996 (page 56: Q ad and Page Hodel's interview); vol.1, issue 2 March 14-April 18, 1996 (page 6-8 Spin it!), issue 2, March 1995, p.23 (the Box); vol. 2, issue 12 Jan.9-Feb 13, 1997 (page 5: The Box ad), vol 21, issue 3 Jan.8-Jan. 22, 1998 (page 17: The Box ad).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Odyssey, premier edition (1992), p. 17 (The Box ad). Odyssey, v.8.issue 13, August 13-26, 1999 (SOUL ad, p. 11). And more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included Page Hodel's interview at the San Francisco Sentinel. "Page Hodel," Spin Doctor at the Metro, Life Style, Summer 1995, p. 36-37. Rewind/march 93-17 with a paragraph talking about gender inequality in the music industry and more. Included articles regarding the San Francisco Chronicle article on The Box, which resulted in an "invasion" of straight people trying to get into the club. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 24, 25, Box 3, Folder 27, Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 29, 51.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series supplied by the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder titles supplied by the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed headshots of musicians Chéna, Chaka Khan, Evelyn "Champagne", King, Jeffrey Osborne, Thelma Houston, Cameon Paul, and Rozalla.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier for The Box 2.0, the new version of The Box after it moved back to its original location in 1998.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier for the event, held on May 21, 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photo-collage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 10, Box 2, Folder 89, Box 5, Folder 24, and Box 14, Folder 10.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included photocopies of apology letters from Hodel to people who were not let into The Box, attached to a cover letter from the Sexual Orientation Discrimination Representative of the City and County of San Francisco's Human Rights Commission. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 24, 25, Box 3, Folder 27, Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 16-17, 51.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A flier for a benefit for the Yes on K campaign on Wednesday, October 10, 1990. Proposition K, which ultimately passed, allowed same-sex couples to register their relationships as domestic partnerships with the city.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of the sign at The Box's entrance and a memorial event for a Box customer who had suffered a heart attack and died while on the dance floor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier for a 1991 benefit to raise money for the documentary "Partners," about the fight for domestic partnership benefits. It was hosted by politician, activist, and comedian Tom Ammiano.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A flier for the event "Shirts with a Difference." Jabez Jr. was a successful African-American clothing designer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: African American disco singer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jay King (1958-2019 ) was a Black gay man and a dancer, doorman, and manager at The Box.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Box's opening night on April 7, 1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the contract. Relevant material can be found in Box 3, Folder 22, Box (The). Queen Latifah, New Year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included the contract and a promotional photograph of Roxanne with DJ Hitman Howie Tee. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folder 58, Real Roxanne.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier for the dance/comedy party held on June 9th 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Free passes, a punch card for free entrance on the 11th visit, a personal ad cut out from a newspaper seeking a dance partner for The Box, photographs of Angela Davis, a color autographed photo of drag queen Tawny Goldand, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drag queen and a beloved customer at the Box.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sweat event. See also Box 5, Folder 10, and Box 14, Folder 10; Girl Pride in Factory; several events at the Q Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Amory, Deborah P. 1996. "Club Q: dancing with (a) difference," in Inventing lesbian cultures in America, pp. 145-160.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handbill reading "Boycott Club Q for Sex Discrimination. We have enough from the outside." According to Hodel, there was never an actual boycott, and "there were probably two people [protesting] because everybody loved us." She believes the protesters were mad because the bouncers sometimes reminded groups of men coming into Club Q that it was a women's space in order to keep it safe for the community. Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 24, 25, Box 3, Folder 27, Box 4, Folders 2, 9, 16-17, 29, 51.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included handwritten letters.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Logos, stationery and T-shirts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-ups of the logo.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Newspaper clippings and articles
Club Q. Prints and copies of fliers and handbills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included "Pride weekend" party, "Queen of hearts", and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a hand-bound book titled "Thank Q", a postcard with a Queen of Hearts card, photographs with groups of people outside the club, a handwritten letter by Kristen Wolffor, a newspaper clipping of a missed connection classified ad for someone met at Club Q, a scissor with Q letters, a Mini Cassette " Margi Adam," and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of stage sets taken by Chloe Atkins. Included also unattributed photographs, and hand-drawn plans for the sets.

did.unittitle: Series IV. Photographs, Slides, and Negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included Page Hodel's photocollages.

did.unittitle: Black and White Photographs with people in front of a Jeep, by Chloe Atkins

scopecontent.p: List of names on the back of both.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included contact sheets. Photographs by Chloe Atkins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 contact sheets.

did.unittitle: Box's Pride Parade float and other events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proof sheets and the negatives. Photographs were used in an ad for a local scooter shop.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contact sheets.

did.unittitle: Club Q, clubgoers and promotional photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by Chloe Atkins and Dawn Lewis.

did.unittitle: Club Q. Bra party I, II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages of lists of people who participated in the photo shoots.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by Chloe Atkins. 5 film strips and 36 proof sheets featuring black and white photographs of customers and dancers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by Chloe Atkins and Dawn Lewis. Similar photographs can be found in Box 3, Folders 5, 13, and 9. Included also photographs from Halloween parties and other shows, a color photograph and a copy of Janet Jackson and others, a promotional card postal from "Que," and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dance Factory staff and Hodel in an AIDS event. Relevant material in Box 6 Memorabilia and Banners by Rive Nestor (MU-4442, MU-4446) in Series Clothes and Banners.

did.unittitle: Kerry and Mona shoot by Chloe Atkins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contact sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related material to the photographer may be found in Jessica Tanzer papers, #7866. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.p: Contact sheets for a story in 1991.

did.unittitle: Slides with Box fliers and Page Hodel's portraits
Sweat Event, Georgio, flower decorated parties

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 10, Box 2, Folder 89, Box 4, Folder 28, and Box 14, Folder 10.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Portraits of Box patrons by Chloe Atkins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black and white and color photographs. The Box had a photo set up by the door, and Atkins took portraits of every customer who came in.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Dancers and DJ Page Hodel
Sweat project presented by Page Hodel
The Box and Club Q events
The Box and Club Q staff and patrons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included several events (Halloween parties, anniversaries, birthday parties, Pride parades, and more). Included photographs by Chloe Atkins, Dawn Lewis, and Photo Graphics Darlene (1987).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Photographs by Photo Graphics Darlene (1987), no reproduction by any means without prior written consent (Box 15, Folder 7).

did.unittitle: Box (The). People, stage sets, and dance floor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of people on the dance floor, Hodel in the DJ booth, bartenders, stage sets, and performers. Included photographs by Chloe Atkins and Dawn Lewis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material can be found in Box 1, Folders 16, 32, Box 2, Folders 16, 50, Box 3, Folder 75, Box 4, Folder 12.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dance Floor event in 1986 and more.

did.unittitle: Box (The). Portraits of Box patrons by Chloe Atkins
Box (The). Portraits of Box patrons by Chloe Atkins

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Photographs arrived stuck in pairs. Please do not try to separate them.

did.unittitle: Black and white photographs from Club Q and the Box. Staff, patrons, and DJ Page Hodel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included photographs taken by Chloe Atkins and and Dawn Lewis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pride parades and other street parties by The Box.

did.unittitle: Portraits and events from Club Q and the Box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included photographs from the Box's participation at the San Francisco Pride parade (10 years).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Researchers can open the bags one at a time, apart from the party kits, which contain confetti that cannot be opened.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Back stage," "Stage," and "Tech Crew" buttons; a commemorative watch for the 1996 Los Angeles Dance-a-thon, 2 Dance-a-thon wristbands.

did.unittitle: Award AC4A Angel Awardee Lifetime Achievement
Awards (statues/plaques) from charities and community organizations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: VIP, entrance, backstage and more.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Party kits containing confetti cannot be opened.

did.unittitle: Bag with matches, cloth wallet, temporary tattoo, a Nat pin and other souvenirs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Back up buttons.

did.unittitle: Barbie Page doll dressed in a Club Q t-shirt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hodel's good friend Gia Giasullo made Barbie Page to represent Page when she would stand with a handful of others out on 18th and Castro handing out flyers, dancing, and drumming up interest in the clubs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamps with the club's address and logo, "The Funk," The Box Dance Club," and "The Wet." Also included 2 plastic letters (X, B), a pen, 3 cloth wallets with condoms, a pocket planner, and a mini plastic record.

did.unittitle: Cable Car Awards plaques for Outstanding DJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Q Nine" (for the 9th anniversary) "Q", "Q" with heart next to it, and a peace sign made into a Q.

did.unittitle: Club Q, a bag of six Q-shaped magnets
Club Q, a bag of twelve mini wooden Qs
Club Q, a bag with a stamp, a mini-scissor, a slide with Queen Hearts card and two Q buttons
Club Q, a cardboard box in the shape of a Q
Club Q, a cut-out paper Qs in a party kid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also Box 3, Folder 55.

did.unittitle: Club Q, a large plastic Q
Club Q, a wooden block with a Q carved on it

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift bags handed out to clubgoers with confetti and small plastic toys. Item MU-4249 included a chocolate kiss and candies, which were photographed and then removed from the bag.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Club Q, Love kit bag contained a chocolate kiss and heart-shaped candies, which were removed. There are digital photographs of the original party kit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant material in Box 1, Folder 47-48; Box 5, Folder 15, Box 6 (Memorabilia, stamp), and Banners by Rive Nestor (MU-4442, MU-4446) in Series Clothes and Banners.

scopecontent.p: Pink letters on a black background by Rive Nestor. Heavy cotton canvas. H.6 feet 6 inches* L 8 feet 7.5 inches.

scopecontent.p: H. 8 feet L. 11 feet 4 inches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yellow and cyan letters on a white background. Heavy cotton canvas. H. 10 feet 1 inch L. 10 feet 11.5 inches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pink letters on a black background. Heavy cotton canvas. H. 9 feet 6 inches * L. 9 feet 3 inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: H. 7 feet * L 7 feet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: T-shirt received torn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page Hodel's professional website documents Page Hodel's professional activities as a dance club DJ, radio Mixtress, and nightclub promoter in the San Francisco Bay Area and beyond. It also covers projects she worked on as an artist, musician, and carpenter, along with house renovation projects.

did.unittitle: Back up 4 old graphics to File 5.99

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disc (5) applications (A).

scopecontent.p: See also Box 21, Folder Velvet.
